name: Cheniere Energy
summary: Largest US LNG exporter (Sabine Pass + Corpus Christi). **CHENIERE $240.85 MAY 22 CLOSE (+0.2% 1D, -6.1% 1M, +8.1% 3M) — LNG-BASIS STRUCTURAL BID INTACT. STRUCTURAL-BID TRIO (XLE-FRONTLINE-CHENIERE-LOCKHEED) VALIDATED.** Per markets dossier C8: Henry Hub $2.91 (-3.7% 1D EIA storage build, +11.2% MoM LNG-basis); TTF €48.69 (-1.46% 1D, +30%+ vs ~€31 baseline) — flat-to-easing but Qatar Ras Laffan FM 17% liquefaction persists as structural floor. Cheniere remains the cleanest non-Iran-conditional long inside the structural-bid trio. **Hormuz reopening (Baker Hughes call: not until 2H26) remains the only structural reverse.** Trump 'every day better' rhetoric and Munir COAS visit do NOT alter the LNG-basis story — Qatar Ras Laffan force majeure is structural-physical, not deal-conditional. **Reversibility**: Crisis-linked component (~25%) reverses on Pakistan-track failure (modest negative); structural LNG-basis (~75%) requires durable Hormuz physical reopening — not the leaked text's freedom-of-navigation clause but actual VLCC/LNG-tanker resumption. PRIOR (May 22): **CHENIERE $239.05 -0.6% 1D / -6.8% 1M / +7.3% 3M — LNG-BASIS STRUCTURAL BID INTACT EVEN ON DEAL-ANTICIPATION TAPE. Q1 DIVIDEND $0.555/SHARE PAID MAY 19; $1.2B DEPLOYED Q1 TOWARD GROWTH/REPURCHASES/DIVIDENDS PER 8-K. STRUCTURAL-BID TRIO (XLE-FRONTLINE-CHENIERE) VALIDATED FOR SECOND CONSECUTIVE CYCLE UPDATE.** **Structural thesis intact**: Q1 capital deployment $1.2B is consistent with sustained capital deployment off elevated EBITDA guide; Qatar Ras Laffan 17% liquefaction force majeure persists; EU storage refill window tightening into summer; ECB Lagarde 'new energy shock' framing carries forward. **The Hormuz reopening (Baker Hughes call: not until 2H26) remains the only structural reverse for this trade.** The paper-vs-physical bifurcation pattern present in Brent ($104.52, tail-risk de-priced) does NOT extend to LNG basis — TTF €47.69/MWh is a SHALLOW retracement (-2%) despite Brent -5.3% across window. **Cheniere is the cleanest non-Iran-conditional long inside the structural-bid trio (XLE + Frontline + Cheniere + Lockheed)** — same pattern as the May 19 brief flagged, now operationally validated for the second cycle update. **Wall Street structural thesis (multi-year US LNG export advantage; EU energy-security cycle)** is NOT vulnerable to Pakistan deal track per se — only to durable Hormuz physical reopening. PRIOR (May 19 — Tuesday): Created 2026-05-19 per markets dossier E1 — appears in 4+ independent sources as transmission mechanism between European TTF gas basis ↔ Hormuz LNG dislocation ↔ US LNG export economics. **Tuesday May 19**: +2.4% to $247.74 on raised 2026 EBITDA guide to $7.25-7.75B (Oilprice / 24/7 Wall St); EU storage 141 cargoes below 5-year low per Cheniere management commentary. **Structural thesis**: with Qatar LNG and Iranian gas off the global market via Hormuz blockade, European emergency-supply needs flow through US LNG exporters; Cheniere is the largest. The Hormuz reopening (Baker Hughes call: not until 2H26) is the only structural reverse for this trade. **Wall Street upgrades** post-EBITDA-guide raise: Cheniere is now positioned as the cleanest non-Iran-conditional long inside the structural-bid trio (XLE, Frontline, Cheniere). Recent moves UP on Brent-down day (+2.4% Tuesday despite Brent -1.6%) confirm war premium has migrated OUT of crude flat-price INTO LNG basis.

price_snapshots.trigger: EBITDA guide raise + structural-bid trio Brent-inverse day
price_snapshots.delta_context: +2.4% on a Brent -1.6% / WTI -4.7% day = war premium migrating out of crude flat-price into LNG basis

active_triggers.condition: Cheniere announces additional EBITDA guide raise within 60 days (by 2026-07-18)
active_triggers.note: Tuesday's $7.25-7.75B guide raise was off pre-war expectations; if European storage builds slower than expected through summer, another raise within 60 days would confirm structural-bid thesis.

active_triggers.condition: Hormuz reopens (Baker Hughes 2H26 timeline) — bearish reversal trigger
active_triggers.note: The only structural reverse for the Cheniere long thesis. Baker Hughes call: not until 2H26. Tracker: vessel transit returning toward ~138/day pre-war benchmark.

recent_signals.headline: **CHENIERE NODE CREATED — +2.4% TO $247.74 ON RAISED 2026 EBITDA GUIDE TO $7.25-7.75B + EU STORAGE 141 CARGOES BELOW 5-YEAR LOW.** Largest US LNG exporter; transmission mechanism between European TTF basis ↔ Hormuz LNG dislocation ↔ US LNG export economics. Cheniere management commentary on European storage 141 cargoes below 5-year low gives structural visibility into European emergency-supply needs. Wall Street upgrades post-EBITDA-guide raise. **Tuesday's +2.4% on a Brent -1.6% / WTI -4.7% day is the cleanest demonstration that war premium has migrated out of crude flat-price into LNG basis** — Cheniere is decoupled from spot oil because cash flows are multi-year contracted offtake, not commodity-linked. Inside the structural-bid trio (XLE, Frontline, Cheniere), Cheniere shares the Hormuz-still-closed structural premium with the other two — all three moved UP on Brent-down day. **The Hormuz reopening (Baker Hughes call: not until 2H26) is the only structural reverse** for this trade.
sources: Cheniere management commentary (European storage 141 cargoes below 5y low)
recent_signals.verification: Cheniere +2.4% = CONFIRMED operational tape; $7.25-7.75B EBITDA guide = CONFIRMED (Cheniere primary + Oilprice + 24/7 Wall St); EU storage 141 cargoes below 5y low = REPORTED (Cheniere management commentary); structural-bid trio inverse-to-Brent move = CONFIRMED operational pattern.
